So it is with the wicked. They are "as the chaff that is driven with the whirlwind out of the floor" (Hosea 13:3; Ps. 1:4; 35:5), or are "led about by Satan, even as chaff is driven before the wind" (Morm. 5:18). (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Donald W. Parry, A Guide to Scriptural Symbols, p.28)

R. Eleazar once went to pay a visit to R. Jose ben R. Simeon ben Lakunia, his father-in-law, accompanied by R. Hiya and R. Jose. On reaching a certain field they sat down under a tree, and R. Eleazar said: ‘Let each of us give a discourse on the Torah.’ He himself commenced with the text: “Yet I am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt, and thou shalt know no god but me” (Hos. 13, 4). He said:…

Blasted with the east wind- It has been very properly observed that all the mischief done to corn or fruit, by blasting, smutting, mildews, locusts, etc., is attributed to the east wind. See Exodus 10:13; 14:21; Psalm 78:26; Ezekiel 17:10; Jon 4:8. In Egypt it is peculiarly destructive, because it comes through the parched deserts of Arabia, often destroying vast numbers of men and women. The…
